fn4: That is, to ﬁnd out the caste they belonged to.
fn8: The arrangement for the marriage would depend on what caste the Pandavas came from.
fn11: The Pandavas. Drupada assured them that he would help them get back their kingdom.
fn18: Dhrishtadyumna. The text is such that it could also mean Drupada, ruler of Panchala.
fn21: Meaning probably Drupada. Parshata is a family name, used for both Drupada and Dhrishtadyumna.
fn31: Indra, the vajra is Indra’s weapon.
fn34: The king of mountains (giriraja) means high and lofty mountains, but is also a name for the Himalayas.
fn36: The other god. As will become clear, this other god was none other than Shiva or Mahadeva.
fn38: Shiva or Mahadeva; literally the lord of the mountains.
fn39: One of Indra’s names; literally the performer of a hundred sacriﬁces.
fn53: Parshata is one of Drupada’s names.
fn60: That is, Bhima got married on the next day, Arjuna on the next, and so on.
fn62: That is, she regained her virginity after the earlier day’s marriage.
